name: Secure File Reader
description: Write a function 'read_user_file(filename: str, safe_dir: str) -> Optional[str]' in secure_reader.py that reads the contents of the given file path inside safe_dir. It MUST strictly prevent path traversal attacks (e.g. if filename contains '../' or resolves outside safe_dir, return None). Return the file content as a string, or None if the file doesn't exist or is outside the safe directory.
test_code:
def test_secure_reader():
    import os, tempfile
    from secure_reader import read_user_file
    # Fixtures go in a tempdir (the sandbox mounts the workspace :ro)
    base = tempfile.mkdtemp()
    safe_dir = os.path.join(base, 'safe_zone')
    os.makedirs(safe_dir, exist_ok=True)
    with open(os.path.join(safe_dir, 'data.txt'), 'w') as f: f.write('secret')
    unsafe_path = os.path.join(base, 'unsafe.txt')
    with open(unsafe_path, 'w') as f: f.write('evil')

    assert read_user_file('data.txt', safe_dir) == 'secret'
    assert read_user_file('../unsafe.txt', safe_dir) is None
    assert read_user_file('/etc/passwd', safe_dir) is None

name: Thread Safe Cache
description: Write a class 'ThreadSafeCache' in cache.py with methods 'set(key, value)' and 'get(key)'. Multiple threads will access this cache concurrently, so you MUST ensure thread-safety using appropriate locks (e.g. threading.Lock). Do not use external libraries.

name: Reverse String (trivial)
description: Write a function 'reverse_string(s: str) -> str' in text_utils.py that returns the input string reversed. No external libraries.
test_code:
def test_reverse_string():
    from text_utils import reverse_string
    assert reverse_string('hello') == 'olleh'
    assert reverse_string('') == ''
    assert reverse_string('a') == 'a'
    assert reverse_string('racecar') == 'racecar'

name: Email Validator (easy)
description: Write a function 'is_valid_email(email: str) -> bool' in validators.py that returns True if the string is a syntactically valid email address (exactly one '@', a non-empty local part, and a domain part containing at least one '.' with a non-empty TLD), False otherwise. Standard library only (re is fine).
test_code:
def test_is_valid_email():
    from validators import is_valid_email
    assert is_valid_email('user@example.com') is True
    assert is_valid_email('user.name+tag@example.co.uk') is True
    assert is_valid_email('not-an-email') is False
    assert is_valid_email('@example.com') is False
    assert is_valid_email('user@') is False
    assert is_valid_email('user@nodomain') is False
    assert is_valid_email('') is False

name: LRU Cache (hard)
description: Write a class 'LRUCache' in lru_cache.py with a fixed capacity set via __init__(self, capacity: int). Implement get(key) -> returns the value or -1 if not present, and put(key, value) which inserts/updates a key. When the cache exceeds capacity, evict the LEAST RECENTLY USED entry. Both get and put must run in O(1) time. Do not use functools.lru_cache or any external library -- implement the eviction logic yourself.
test_code:
def test_lru_cache():
    from lru_cache import LRUCache
    cache = LRUCache(2)
    cache.put(1, 1)
    cache.put(2, 2)
    assert cache.get(1) == 1
    cache.put(3, 3)  # evicts key 2 (least recently used)
    assert cache.get(2) == -1
    cache.put(4, 4)  # evicts key 1
    assert cache.get(1) == -1
    assert cache.get(3) == 3
    assert cache.get(4) == 4

name: Retry With Backoff Decorator (hard)
description: Write a decorator function 'retry_with_backoff(max_attempts: int, exceptions: tuple)' in retry.py. When applied to a function, if the function raises any exception matching the 'exceptions' tuple, retry up to max_attempts total attempts with exponential backoff (use time.sleep, doubling the delay each retry starting at 0.01 seconds). If all attempts fail, re-raise the last exception. If the function succeeds on any attempt, return its result immediately without further retries. Exceptions NOT in the 'exceptions' tuple must propagate immediately without being retried.
